title: Archive transaction: writeCell boundary serialization, 4 missing archived guards, journal + collision preflight
action: skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/cells.mjs (amended by advisor consult — writeCell MUST STAY SYNCHRONOUS, no async cascade; call-site cells.mjs:859 maps writeCell synchronously and must keep working unchanged): (1) writeCell does a SYNC single-attempt O_EXCL acquire of the cells-archive lockfile applying the same stale rules synchronously; on contention (a live archive/unarchive transaction) it throws typed CELLS_ARCHIVE_BUSY — callers under withStoreLock(cells:<id>) surface it; archive transactions are short so busy is rare and honest. Inside the held lock: if the cell file exists in the archive tree but NOT at the active path, throw CELL_ARCHIVED instead of resurrecting; then write; then release. Lock order stays cells:<id> -> cells-archive; archiveFeature/unarchiveFeature never take cells:<id> and never call writeCell (verified: they renameSync directly) — no deadlock, no re-entrancy. Fix the false comment at L565-569. (2) Add assertNotArchived to reopenCell, setTier, resetCellBudget, recordJudgeVerdict (same call the other six mutators use). (3) archiveFeature: preflight destination collisions BEFORE any rename (typed refusal, never overwrite); write a journal file (.bee/cells/archive/<feature>/.journal.json listing planned moves) before the first rename, delete it after the summary write succeeds; summary write moves inside the guarded section; at archiveFeature AND unarchiveFeature entry, an existing journal rolls the interrupted transaction back before proceeding. (4) unarchiveFeature: refuse to overwrite an existing active destination (typed error). Tests in templates/tests/test_cells.mjs: mutator write after archive rename throws CELL_ARCHIVED (not resurrect); the 4 newly guarded mutators refuse an archived cell; collision preflight refuses; journal rollback recovers a simulated half-archive; unarchive refuses over an existing active file; writeCell during a held cells-archive lock throws CELLS_ARCHIVE_BUSY.

truths: No mutator can write an active copy of a cell whose file lives in the archive tree
A simulated half-archive (journal present, some renames done) is rolled back cleanly on next archive/unarchive entry
prohibitions: Do not take cells:<id> inside archiveFeature (lock-order inversion)
Do not change readCell archive fallback semantics for reads (deps/reviews rely on it)

trace.outcome: Serialized writeCell against the cells-archive lock (sync, single-attempt, typed CELLS_ARCHIVE_BUSY on contention; final archived-only check under the lock throws CELL_ARCHIVED instead of resurrecting); added assertNotArchived to reopenCell/setTier/resetCellBudget/recordJudgeVerdict; archiveFeature/unarchiveFeature now preflight destination collisions before any rename, write a journal before the first rename and clear it after the commit point, and both recover an interrupted prior transaction's journal at entry; unarchiveFeature refuses to overwrite an existing active file; fixed the false serialization comment at archiveFeature's top. 8 new/updated tests in test_cells.mjs, all green.
deviations: Touched skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/lock.mjs (not in the cell's declared `files` list) to add an exported acquireStoreLockOnceSync — writeCell's SYNC single-attempt O_EXCL acquire needed a primitive lock.mjs didn't have; lock.mjs was already in the cell's read_first list. No existing export/behavior in lock.mjs changed.

unarchiveFeature's journal-clear ordering: cleared the journal right after the rename loop succeeds (before the rmdir + summary-delete), rather than strictly after the summary write as archiveFeature does — the journal lives inside archiveDir, so rmdir cannot succeed while it is still present, and once every rename has landed there is nothing left for the journal to protect.

Fixed the false comment on archiveFeature's top-level lock (explicitly requested by the cell action text): it claimed a concurrent mutator could never interleave (false pre-fix — mutators held no lock against archive at all) and implied per-feature scoping (false — the 'cells-archive' lock name is global across all features).
